segments.text: Now concerning things offered to idols:
We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love edifies.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he knows nothing yet as he ought to know.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But if anyone loves God, this one is known by Him.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore concerning the eating of things offered to idols,
We know that an idol is nothing in the world, and that there is no other God but one.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For even if there are so-called gods, whether in heaven or on earth (as there are many gods and many lords),
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Yet for us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for Him; and one Lord Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and through whom we live.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: However, there is not in everyone that knowledge; for some, with consciousness of the idol, until now eat it as a thing offered to an idol; and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But food does not commend us to God; for neither if we eat are we the better, nor if we do not eat are we the worse.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But beware lest somehow this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to those who are weak.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For if anyone sees you who have knowledge eating in an idol's temple, will not the conscience of him who is weak be emboldened to eat those things offered to idols?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And because of your knowledge shall the weak brother perish, for whom Christ died?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But when you thus sin against the brethren, and wound their weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore, if food makes my brother stumble, I will never again eat meat, lest I make my brother stumble.
segments.speaker: Paul
